title: malus × soulardii
intro: Malus × soulardii, also known as the Soulard crabapple, is a hybrid species of apple tree that is native to North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Malus × soulardii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in folk medicine.
